title: Sweet Dee Has a Heart Attack

jokes.text: Go download me a hoagie off the Internet.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to use technology/internet terminology
jokes.punchline: Treating the internet like a food delivery service with impossible verb usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank absurdism - completely nonsensical but delivered with confidence. Great economy and character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Go download me a hoagie off the Internet.

jokes.text: Those words don't make any s- sense.
jokes.setup: Frank's nonsensical internet hoagie request
jokes.punchline: Dee's stammering confusion trying to process the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that enhances the absurdity of Frank's line. The stammer adds realism to the confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Those words don't make any s- sense.

jokes.text: There's pigeons in the bar.
jokes.setup: Dee is having chest pains and needs help
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete non-sequitur about pigeons instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie-ism - his brain works completely differently. Great timing and character integration during a crisis moment.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There's pigeons in the bar.

jokes.text: I didn't know women could have heart attacks.
jokes.setup: Dee just had a heart attack and is explaining the situation
jokes.punchline: Mac's complete ignorance about basic medical facts
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's ignorance and complete lack of empathy. Simple but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I didn't know women could have heart attacks.

jokes.text: Dee, your ship has sailed. Okay, it's time to move on to us, the people who are going to live on.
jokes.setup: Dee wants sympathy for her heart attack
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismissing her completely and focusing on himself
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism - turning someone else's medical emergency into being about him. Great cringe factor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee, your ship has sailed. Okay, it's time to move on to us, the people who are going to live on.

jokes.text: Or a cop to shoot a guy
jokes.setup: Mac saying you don't pay a fireman to put out a fire
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dark completion of the analogy
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on a simple analogy that's very Charlie. Unexpected and subversive.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Or a cop to shoot a guy

jokes.text: I do always give a fake name 'cause I like to stay off the grid. They usually just give me a bunch of antibiotics, the sores go away, and I walk out.
jokes.setup: Discussion about hospital payment
jokes.punchline: Charlie's revealing his sketchy hospital habits and mysterious sores
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie character moment - paranoid, gross, and oddly proud of gaming the system.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I do always give a fake name 'cause I like to stay off the grid. They usually just give me a bunch of antibiotics, the sores go away, and I walk out.

jokes.text: Really lets your ass breathe.
jokes.setup: Charlie wearing a hospital gown
jokes.punchline: His weird enthusiasm about the gown's ventilation properties
jokes.explanation: Charlie finding weird joy in the mundane. The phrase 'ass breathe' becomes a running element.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Really lets your ass breathe.

jokes.text: I canceled that shit when you were nine.
jokes.setup: Kids expecting to be on their father's insurance policy
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing he canceled it decades ago without telling them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank - completely selfish and casual about screwing over his kids. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I canceled that shit when you were nine.

jokes.text: Well, get out.
jokes.setup: Long discussion about insurance coverage
jokes.punchline: Doctor's blunt, immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. The bluntness after the buildup works well.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Well, get out.

jokes.text: Why is the government not providing us with health insurance? I mean, what is this, like some kind of socialist country or some kind of communist dictatorship?
jokes.setup: Being denied healthcare due to no insurance
jokes.punchline: Dennis's complete backwards understanding of political systems
jokes.explanation: Dennis's political ignorance is funny but the joke is a bit verbose and obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why is the government not providing us with health insurance? I mean, what is this, like some kind of socialist country or some kind of communist dictatorship?

jokes.text: How does an ass breathe? Explain that to me.
jokes.setup: Charlie keeps saying his ass can breathe in the gown
jokes.punchline: Mac's literal confusion about the impossible biological claim
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on Charlie's weird phrase. Mac's literal thinking plays well.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: How does an ass breathe? Explain that to me.

jokes.text: I can't sift through the duds. I got to take 'em all because I got to get healthy really fast.
jokes.setup: Frank has lots of prescription pills
jokes.punchline: His completely backwards logic about taking all medications at once
jokes.explanation: Frank's impatient, reckless approach to health. The logic is perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I can't sift through the duds. I got to take 'em all because I got to get healthy really fast.

jokes.text: I want to get that Jesus-on-the-cross look. I think that crucifixion must have been really good for your core because... Absolutely. Jesus had, like, the best abs.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting lean and fit
jokes.punchline: Treating crucifixion as a workout routine and Jesus as fitness inspiration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and original. Taking something sacred and making it about vanity fitness is peak Sunny subversiveness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I want to get that Jesus-on-the-cross look. I think that crucifixion must have been really good for your core because...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Absolutely. Jesus had, like, the best abs.

jokes.text: I want to get that Jesus-on-the-cross look. I think that crucifixion must have been really good for your core because... Absolutely. Jesus had, like, the best abs.
jokes.setup: Discussing fitness goals and body types
jokes.punchline: Treating crucifixion as an effective workout routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly blasphemous fitness advice delivered with complete sincerity. Original, shocking, and perfectly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I want to get that Jesus-on-the-cross look. I think that crucifixion must have been really good for your core because...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Absolutely. Jesus had, like, the best abs.

jokes.text: Mac and Dennis immediately abandon workout plans to get supplements and outfits instead
jokes.setup: Two guys pumped up to start exercising
jokes.punchline: Immediately find excuses to avoid actual work
jokes.explanation: Perfect characterization of their shallow commitment to health. Visual comedy of them walking away from equipment.

dialogue.line: Mac and Dennis immediately abandon workout plans to get supplements and outfits instead

jokes.text: Well, we thought we'd save paper. You know, go green.
jokes.setup: Interviewer noting they've never seen two people share a resume
jokes.punchline: Mac's environmental excuse for their obviously unprofessional approach
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - making their laziness sound noble. Decent structure but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, we thought we'd save paper. You know, go green.

jokes.text: For several years, I've been in complete charge of pretty much everything in my life... There's certain things that you just can't have control of, sir.
jokes.setup: Reading resume qualifications
jokes.punchline: Charlie's meaningless job description that applies to everyone, followed by his philosophical qualifier
jokes.explanation: Charlie's weird brain trying to sound professional. The qualifier makes it even more absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: For several years, I've been in complete charge of pretty much everything in my life... There's certain things that you just can't have control of, sir.

jokes.text: This has nothing to do with money. This is sort of a health insurance situation for us... so, you know, frankly, you don't even really have to pay us at all.
jokes.setup: Negotiating salary for the job
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete honesty that undermines their negotiating position
jokes.explanation: Charlie's inability to lie or play the game. Good cringe factor as he destroys their leverage.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This has nothing to do with money. This is sort of a health insurance situation for us... so, you know, frankly, you don't even really have to pay us at all.

jokes.text: Well, maybe you shouldn't dress like a bumblebee, bitch.
jokes.setup: Spin instructor telling Dee she shouldn't be in class after a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Dee's petty insult about the instructor's workout attire
jokes.explanation: Good Dee pettiness. The bumblebee comparison is visual and mean in her signature style.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you shouldn't dress like a bumblebee, bitch.

jokes.text: You just dogged, bro. You just got dogged.
jokes.setup: Dee insulted the spin instructor
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis's juvenile excitement about the insult
jokes.explanation: Weak reaction beat that's pretty predictable. Shows their immaturity but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You just dogged, bro.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You just got dogged.

jokes.text: Spin class. A bunch of hamsters on a wheel. I'm gonna ride a bike hard, I'm gonna ride a bike fast and oh, yeah, I'm not gonna go anywhere.
jokes.setup: Getting kicked out of spin class
jokes.punchline: Dee's rant about the pointlessness of stationary bikes
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor but fairly obvious. The hamster comparison is the strongest part.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Spin class. A bunch of hamsters on a wheel. I'm gonna ride a bike hard, I'm gonna ride a bike fast and oh, yeah, I'm not gonna go anywhere.

jokes.text: I, on the other hand, have not taken a shit in days... My body's working at 100% efficiency. My body is absorbing every single nutrient, and it's not wasting a single thing.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking about explosive diarrhea from supplements
jokes.punchline: Mac's completely backwards interpretation of severe constipation as peak health
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Mac logic - turning a medical problem into proof of superiority. Perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I, on the other hand, have not taken a shit in days... My body's working at 100% efficiency. My body is absorbing every single nutrient, and it's not wasting a single thing.

jokes.text: My body is absorbing every single nutrient, and it's not wasting a single thing. My body's working at 100% efficiency.
jokes.setup: Mac hasn't defecated in days due to supplements
jokes.punchline: Interpreting dangerous constipation as peak bodily performance
jokes.explanation: Mac's pseudoscientific rationalization of obvious health problem. His confidence sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: My body is absorbing every single nutrient, and it's not wasting a single thing. My body's working at 100% efficiency.

jokes.text: Mr. Taylor whose grandpa was a sailor. Mr. Scott who drinks his coffee hot. Mr. White whose wife is not too bright.
jokes.setup: Charlie doing his mailroom job
jokes.punchline: His bizarre system of remembering people through nonsensical rhyming attributes
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie weirdness. His brain creates these random associations that make no logical sense but are perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Mr. Taylor whose grandpa was a sailor. Mr. Scott who drinks his coffee hot. Mr. White whose wife is not too bright.

jokes.text: I can't rember... He bangs that old lady, and then they play that song from the '80s. 'Day Bow Bow.'
jokes.setup: Charlie asking how The Secret of My Success ends
jokes.punchline: Mac's garbled, simplified plot summary ending in the nonsensical 'Day Bow Bow'
jokes.explanation: 'Day Bow Bow' is completely nonsensical but perfectly Mac. The plot summary is hilariously reductive.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I can't rember... He bangs that old lady, and then they play that song from the '80s. 'Day Bow Bow.'

jokes.text: What the hell is 'Day Bow Bow'?
jokes.setup: Mac's nonsensical song reference
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confused reaction to Mac's made-up song name
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that highlights the absurdity of Mac's reference. Simple but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What the hell is 'Day Bow Bow'?

jokes.text: The mail goes into three sections according to how important I think the thing looks... Now, the least important stuff, I'm gonna burn that. If it's important, they're gonna send it again, right?
jokes.setup: Mac asking about Charlie's mail system
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely backwards and destructive mail sorting logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - creating an elaborate system that defeats its own purpose. Great character work.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The mail goes into three sections according to how important I think the thing looks... Now, the least important stuff, I'm gonna burn that. If it's important, they're gonna send it again, right?

jokes.text: Charlie smoking and drinking coffee to calm his nerves while sorting mail
jokes.setup: Charlie stressed about mail sorting job
jokes.punchline: Using stimulants to 'calm down'
jokes.explanation: Visual irony of using stimulants to relax. Charlie's backwards logic made physical.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie smoking and drinking coffee to calm his nerves while sorting mail

jokes.text: If you know Latin, you know, like, three languages, so... I don't speak Latin, but there's pictures in here, so I think we're good.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to read Spanish instructions for cosmetic procedure
jokes.punchline: Claiming Latin knowledge he doesn't have, then falling back on pictures
jokes.explanation: Dennis's fake intellectualism immediately contradicted. His confidence in ignorance is the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If you know Latin, you know, like, three languages, so... I don't speak Latin, but there's pictures in here, so I think we're good.

jokes.text: Maybe I put some collagen in your eye. You injected Mexican collagen in my eye?!
jokes.setup: Dee giving Dennis facial injections
jokes.punchline: The horrifying realization she used the wrong substance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with physical consequences. The casual revelation of medical malpractice is very Sunny.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Maybe I put some collagen in your eye.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You injected Mexican collagen in my eye?!

jokes.text: Pepe Silvia... This name keeps coming up over and over again. Every day, Pepe's mail's getting sent back to me... There is no Pepe Silvia. The man does not exist!
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his investigation into the mail
jokes.punchline: His elaborate conspiracy theory built around a name that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Legendary Charlie moment. The escalating paranoia, the mystery name, the conspiracy logic - all perfectly executed. Became a cultural touchstone.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Pepe Silvia... This name keeps coming up over and over again. Every day, Pepe's mail's getting sent back to me... There is no Pepe Silvia. The man does not exist!

jokes.text: There's not a single, goddamn desk in that office. There is no Carol in HR. Mac, half the employees in this building have been made up.
jokes.setup: Charlie's investigation continues
jokes.punchline: The conspiracy expands to include non-existent employees and offices
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the conspiracy. Charlie's paranoid logic building to complete breakdown of reality.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There's not a single, goddamn desk in that office. There is no Carol in HR. Mac, half the employees in this building have been made up.

jokes.text: There's not a single, goddamn desk in that office. There is no Carol in HR.
jokes.setup: Charlie's conspiracy deepening
jokes.punchline: Charlie discovering 'Carol' (Care of) doesn't exist either
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Charlie misreading 'Care of' as 'Carol.' The conspiracy deepens perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There's not a single, goddamn desk in that office. There is no Carol in HR.

jokes.text: You don't see Bar...? Oh, shit, where the hell did he...?
jokes.setup: Charlie referring to Barney who tipped him off
jokes.punchline: Realizing the person he's been talking to doesn't exist or has vanished
jokes.explanation: Great continuation of Charlie's mental breakdown. The moment of him realizing his hallucination is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You don't see Bar...? Oh, shit, where the hell did he...?

jokes.text: Barney. He's the guy who tipped me off to Pepe Silvia. Barney?! Who the hell is Barney?! You don't see Bar...? Oh, shit, where the hell did he...? You've lost your mind. You've lost your goddamn mind, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Charlie referencing another person helping his investigation
jokes.punchline: Realization that Barney is also imaginary
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Charlie's mental break. The moment of realization followed by continued confusion is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Barney. He's the guy who tipped me off to Pepe Silvia.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Barney?! Who the hell is Barney?!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You don't see Bar...? Oh, shit, where the hell did he...?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You've lost your mind. You've lost your goddamn mind, Charlie.

jokes.text: Pick this up and throw it through this window. Pick... throw.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to escape with confused person
jokes.punchline: His simple, caveman-like instructions for breaking out
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment showing his crude problem-solving. Economy of language works well.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Pick this up and throw it through this window. Pick... throw.
